message:
feat(protocol_recovery): tick 207 execute_with_cx per-attempt cx checkpoint (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

ProtocolRecoveryEngine::execute_with_cx already had:
  - pre-flight cx.is_cancel_requested() check at entry
  - between-attempt sleep_with_cx that surfaces cancel as
    RecoveryError::Cancelled

But there was no per-attempt cancel observation at the top of the
retry loop. If cx was cancelled DURING the operation(attempt).await
call (before any error was returned), the outer caller would have
to wait for that operation to complete before the next iteration's
sleep_with_cx observed the cancel.

More importantly: on the final attempt (attempt + 1 == max_attempts),
the between-attempt sleep is skipped, so the loop would just exit
with RetriesExhausted — cancel would be invisible.

Tick 207 adds a top-of-loop `cx.is_cancel_requested()` check. A
cancel fired during the previous operation now lands AT THE ATTEMPT
BOUNDARY rather than after the operation completes, and final-attempt
cancel is now observed correctly.

Attempts field reports the completed-attempt count (not +1) to match
the semantics of legitimate early exits.

Legacy execute() (L548) non-cx retry loop is unchanged — no cx
parameter there.

Verification: 88 / 88 protocol_recovery:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target), including:
- engine_retries_transient
- engine_exhausts_retries
- engine_outcome_error_kinds_populated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
